title: tensor-defi: Tensor Structure in Cryptocurrency Microstructure v1.0.0
description: This proof-of-concept demonstrates tensor decomposition methods applied to cryptocurrency market microstructure, preserving multi-dimensional interaction effects lost by traditional matrix methods. Using one year of hourly OHLCV data for BTC, ETH, and SOL from Binance, rank-4 CP and Tucker decompositions achieve 96.55% and 96.56% explained variance respectively, outperforming PCA (92.31%) by 4.6 percentage points. Version 1.0.0 establishes feasibility on single-venue data for future multi-venue extensions.
references: Kolda, T. G., & Bader, B. W. (2009). Tensor decompositions and applications. SIAM review, 51(3), 455-500.
Lettau, M., & Pelger, M. (2024). Factors That Fit the Time Series and Cross-Section of Stock Returns. The Review of Financial Studies, 37(7), 2193-2228.
Babii, A., Ghysels, E., & Striaukas, J. (2023). Machine learning time series regressions with an application to nowcasting. Journal of Business & Economic Statistics, 41(3), 768-778.
notes: Version 1.0.0: Single-venue proof-of-concept on Binance data with 3 assets (BTC, ETH, SOL). Future work will expand to multi-venue analysis, robustness validation, higher-dimensional features, and information geometry framework. Software includes complete reproducibility pipeline: data collection, tensor construction, CP/Tucker decomposition, PCA baselines, and visualization tools. Expected runtime: 30-90 seconds on consumer-grade hardware.
